questions.q: Dadabhai Naoroji is popularly remembered by which honorific title?
options: The Grand Old Man of India
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji was widely known as the 'Grand Old Man of India' for his long service to the nationalist cause.

questions.q: Which economic theory of colonial exploitation is most closely associated with Dadabhai Naoroji?
questions.explanation: Naoroji developed the influential 'Drain of Wealth' theory, arguing that British rule drained India's resources.

questions.q: In 1892 Dadabhai Naoroji became the first Indian elected to which body?
questions.explanation: In 1892 Naoroji was elected to the British House of Commons, becoming the first Indian to sit there.

questions.q: Dadabhai Naoroji was one of the founders of which major political organization?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was among the founders of the Indian National Congress, established in 1885.

questions.q: To which religious community did Dadabhai Naoroji belong?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was a Parsi, born into a Zoroastrian family in Bombay.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Dadabhai Naoroji born in 1825?
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji was born in Bombay (present-day Mumbai) in 1825.

questions.q: Dadabhai Naoroji's famous book on India's economic condition was titled 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in ___'?
questions.explanation: Naoroji's landmark book was titled 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India.'

questions.q: What was Dadabhai Naoroji's profession before he entered public and political life?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was a professor of mathematics and natural philosophy at Elphinstone College in Bombay.

questions.q: Approximately how many times did Dadabhai Naoroji serve as President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Naoroji presided over the Congress three times, in 1886, 1893, and 1906.

questions.q: Dadabhai Naoroji argued that British rule caused a continuous 'drain' of India's what to England?
questions.explanation: His 'Drain of Wealth' theory held that India's wealth and resources were being siphoned to Britain.

questions.q: For which British constituency was Dadabhai Naoroji elected to the House of Commons in 1892?
questions.explanation: Naoroji won the Central Finsbury seat in London as a Liberal Party candidate in 1892.

questions.q: Under which British political party's banner did Dadabhai Naoroji win his 1892 parliamentary seat?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was elected as a Liberal Party candidate for Central Finsbury in 1892.

questions.q: Which Bombay-based political body, an early forerunner of organized nationalism, did Dadabhai Naoroji help found in 1885?
questions.explanation: Naoroji co-founded the Bombay Presidency Association in 1885 alongside Pherozeshah Mehta and others.

questions.q: Dadabhai Naoroji founded the East India Association in 1866 in which city?
questions.explanation: Naoroji founded the East India Association in London in 1866 to present Indian views to British audiences.

questions.q: At which Congress session did Dadabhai Naoroji, as president, formally adopt 'swaraj' or self-government as the goal?
questions.explanation: At the 1906 Calcutta session, President Naoroji declared 'swaraj' or self-government to be the Congress's aim.

questions.q: Naoroji's remark in the Commons that he was 'a black man' came in a famously dismissive comment by which British Prime Minister about his candidacy?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Lord Salisbury's reference to a 'black man' candidate drew attention to Naoroji and arguably aided his win.

questions.q: Before his political career, Naoroji partnered in establishing one of the first Indian-owned commercial firms in Britain dealing chiefly in what?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was a partner in Cama & Co. and later his own cotton-trading firm in London.

questions.q: Naoroji is regarded as a political mentor and influence on which younger nationalist who later became a key Congress leader?
questions.explanation: Gokhale was deeply influenced by Naoroji's moderate, constitutionalist approach to nationalism.

questions.q: In which year did Dadabhai Naoroji pass away?
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji died in 1917 in Bombay at the age of 91.

questions.q: Naoroji's economic writings argued that India's poverty was largely a result of which policy under colonial rule?
options: The transfer of India's wealth to Britain
questions.explanation: Naoroji attributed Indian poverty primarily to the systematic drain of wealth from India to Britain.

questions.q: Naoroji co-founded and edited which early Anglo-Gujarati periodical to spread reformist and nationalist ideas?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was associated with 'Rast Goftar,' an Anglo-Gujarati journal promoting social reform and awareness.

questions.q: Naoroji served briefly as Diwan (chief minister) of which princely state?
questions.explanation: Naoroji served as Diwan of the princely state of Baroda for a short period in the 1870s.

questions.q: Naoroji is credited with one of the earliest published estimates of India's annual per-capita income, which he placed at roughly how much?
questions.explanation: Naoroji estimated India's per-capita income at about 20 rupees a year to underline the country's poverty.

questions.q: Naoroji first contested a British parliamentary election in 1886, standing unsuccessfully for which constituency?
questions.explanation: Naoroji stood for Holborn in 1886 but lost, before winning Central Finsbury in 1892.

questions.q: Naoroji's analysis of the 'drain' drew partly on official trade data showing a persistent imbalance in India's what?
questions.explanation: Naoroji highlighted India's export surplus that brought no corresponding return, evidence of wealth being drained away.

questions.q: Naoroji presided over the very first session of which body besides the Indian National Congress, related to Indians in Britain?
questions.explanation: Naoroji helped found and lead the London Indian Society, an organization of Indians in Britain.

questions.q: Which term did Naoroji use in the subtitle of his major work to describe the misgovernment he criticized?
questions.explanation: His book was titled 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India,' using 'Un-British' to appeal to British liberal principles.

questions.q: Naoroji is sometimes counted among the trio of founding 'moderates' alongside Pherozeshah Mehta and which other leader?
questions.explanation: Naoroji, Pherozeshah Mehta, and Dinshaw Wacha formed a prominent trio of early Bombay moderates.

questions.q: During his time in Parliament, Naoroji notably pressed for holding the Indian Civil Service examinations where?
questions.explanation: Naoroji campaigned for ICS exams to be held simultaneously in India and England to give Indians a fairer chance.

questions.q: Naoroji's standing as the senior statesman of Indian nationalism led even Mahatma Gandhi to refer to him with which term of respect?
options: The Grand Old Man / a father figure of the nation
questions.explanation: Gandhi and others revered Naoroji as the Grand Old Man, a venerable father figure of the national movement.
